class=\"vector-menu-content-list\">\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\t\n\t\t</ul>\n\t\t\n\t</div>\n</div>\n\n\t\t\t\t\n\t</div>\n</div>\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</nav>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t<div id=\"right-navigation\" class=\"vector-collapsible\">\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t<nav aria-label=\"Views\">\n\t\t\t    \n<div id=\"p-views\" class=\"vector-menu vector-menu-tabs mw-portlet class=\"vector-menu-content-list\">\n\t\t\t\n\t\t\t<li id=\"t-wikibase\" class=\"wb-otherproject-link wb-otherproject-wikibase-dataitem mw-list-item\"><a href=\"https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityPage/Q136214166\" title=\"Structured data on this page hosted by Wikidata [g]\" accesskey=\"g\"><span>Wikidata item</span></a></li>\n\t\t</ul>\n\t\t\n\t</div>\n</div>\n\n</div>\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t\n\t</div>\n</div>\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</nav>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t<div class=\"vector-column-end no-font-mode-scale\">\n\t\t\t\t\t<div class=\"vector-sticky-pinned-container\">\n\t\t\t\t\t\t<div class=\"wp25eastereggs-vector-sitenotice-landmark\"></div>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t<nav class=\"vector-page-tools-landmark\" aria-label=\"Page tools\">\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t<div id=\"vector-page-tools-pinned-container\" class=\"vector-pinned-container\">\n\t\t\t\t\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t</nav>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t<nav class=\"vector-appearance-landmark\" aria-label=\"Appearance\">\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t<div id=\"vector-appearance-pinned-container\" class=\"vector-pinned-container\">\n\t\t\t\t<div id=\"vector-appearance\" class=\"vector-appearance vector-pinnable-element\">\n\t<div class=\"vector-pinnable-header vector-appearance-pinnable-header vector-pinnable-header-pinned\" data-feature-name=\"appearance-pinned\" data-pinnable-element-id=\"vector-appearance\" data-pinned-container-id=\"vector-appearance-pinned-container\" data-unpinned-container-id=\"vector-appearance-unpinned-container\">\n\t<div class=\"vector-pinnable-header-label\">Appearance</div>\n\t<button class=\"vector-pinnable-header-toggle-button vector-pinnable-header-pin-button\" data-event-name=\"pinnable-header.vector-appearance.pin\">move to sidebar</button>\n\t<button class=\"vector-pinnable-header-toggle-button vector-pinnable-header-unpin-button\" data-event-name=\"pinnable-header.vector-appearance.unpin\">hide</button>\n</div>\n\n\n</div>\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t</nav>\n\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t<div id=\"bodyContent\" class=\"vector-body\" aria-labelledby=\"firstHeading\" data-mw-ve-target-container=\"\">\n\t\t\t\t\t<div class=\"vector-body-before-content\">\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t<div class=\"mw-indicators\">\n\t\t</div>\n\n\t\t\t\t\t\t<div id=\"siteSub\" class=\"noprint\">From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia</div>\n\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t\t<div id=\"contentSub\"><div id=\"mw-content-subtitle\"></div></div>\n\t\t\t\t\t\n\t\t\t\t\t\n\t\t\t\t\t<div id=\"mw-content-text\" class=\"mw-body-content\"><div class=\"mw-subjectpageheader\">\n</div><div class=\"mw-content-ltr mw-parser-output\" lang=\"en\" dir=\"ltr\"><div class=\"shortdescription nomobile noexcerpt noprint searchaux\" style=\"display:none\">Calculation of football teams before tournament</div>\n<p class=\"mw-empty-elt\">\n\n</p>\n<figure class=\"mw-default-size\" typeof=\"mw:File/Thumb\"><a href=\"/wiki/File:P202512005DT-1038_President_Donald_Trump_participates_in_the_FIFA_World_Cup_drawing.jpg\" class=\"mw-file-description\"><img alt=\"World Cup draw on December 5, 2025\" src=\"//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/P202512005DT-1038_President_Donald_Trump_participates_in_the_FIFA_World_Cup_drawing.jpg/250px-P202512005DT-1038_President_Donald_Trump_participates_in_the_FIFA_World_Cup_drawing.jpg\" decoding=\"async\" width=\"250\" height=\"167\" class=\"mw-file-element\" srcset=\"//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/P202512005DT-1038_President_Donald_Trump_participates_in_the_FIFA_World_Cup_drawing.jpg/500px-P202512005DT-1038_President_Donald_Trump_participates_in_the_FIFA_World_Cup_drawing.jpg 2x\" data-file-width=\"3000\" data-file-height=\"2000\"></a><figcaption>World Cup draw on December 5, 2025</figcaption></figure>\n<p>The draw for the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup\">2026 FIFA World Cup</a> was held at 12:00&nbsp;<a href=\"/wiki/UTC%E2%88%9205:00\" title=\"UTC−05:00\">UTC−5</a> (EST) on December 5, 2025, at the <a href=\"/wiki/John_F._Kennedy_Center_for_the_Performing_Arts\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts\">John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts</a> in <a href=\"/wiki/Washington,_D.C.\" title=\"Washington, D.C.\">Washington, D.C.</a>, United States. It set the stage for the round-robin group stage in Canada, Mexico, and the United States, where the World Cup will be played. The teams were divided into four pots of twelve, with one team selected from each pot to form a group.\n</p><p>As hosts, <a href=\"/wiki/Mexico_men%27s_national_football_team\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"Mexico men's national football team\">Mexico</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Canada_men%27s_national_soccer_team\" title=\"Canada men's national soccer team\">Canada</a>, and the <a href=\"/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_soccer_team\" title=\"United States men's national soccer team\">United States</a> were seeded. They took positions A1, B1, and D1, respectively, from Pot 1, where they were joined by the top nine qualified teams in the FIFA rankings. The countries occupying positions 13 to 24 on the ranking of the qualified teams were allocated to Pot 2, while the 25th to 36th best-ranked qualifiers were placed in Pot 3. Finally, Pot 4 included the qualified teams in positions 37 to 42, plus six placeholders – two for the winners of the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\">inter-confederation playoffs</a>, and four for the winners of the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_UEFA_second_round\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA second round\">European playoffs</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-1\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-1\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>1<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The final six teams to qualify were determined in these playoffs played on March 26 and 31, 2026, during the <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_International_Match_Calendar\" title=\"FIFA International Match Calendar\">FIFA International Match Calendar</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-2\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-2\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>2<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<meta property=\"mw:PageProp/toc\">\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Ceremony\">Ceremony</h2><span class=\"mw-editsection\"><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">[</span><a href=\"/w/index.php?title=2026_FIFA_World_Cup_draw&amp;action=edit&amp;section=1\" title=\"Edit section: Ceremony\"><span>edit</span></a><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">]</span></span></div>\n<p>The draw ceremony was hosted by <a href=\"/wiki/Kevin_Hart\" title=\"Kevin Hart\">Kevin Hart</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Heidi_Klum\" title=\"Heidi Klum\">Heidi Klum</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Danny_Ramirez\" title=\"Danny Ramirez\">Danny Ramirez</a>. The ceremony also featured live performances from <a href=\"/wiki/Andrea_Bocelli\" title=\"Andrea Bocelli\">Andrea Bocelli</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Lauryn_Hill\" title=\"Lauryn Hill\">Lauryn Hill</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Nicole_Scherzinger\" title=\"Nicole Scherzinger\">Nicole Scherzinger</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Robbie_Williams\" title=\"Robbie Williams\">Robbie Williams</a>. In addition, <a href=\"/wiki/Village_People\" title=\"Village People\">Village People</a> performed their song \"<a href=\"/wiki/Y.M.C.A._(song)\" title=\"Y.M.C.A. (song)\">Y.M.C.A.</a>\" following the conclusion of the draw.<sup id=\"cite_ref-3\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-3\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>3<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The draw itself was conducted by <a href=\"/wiki/Rio_Ferdinand\" title=\"Rio Ferdinand\">Rio Ferdinand</a>, with Samantha Johnson as co-conductor and <a href=\"/wiki/Eli_Manning\" title=\"Eli Manning\">Eli Manning</a> as the red carpet host. <a href=\"/wiki/Tom_Brady\" title=\"Tom Brady\">Tom Brady</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Wayne_Gretzky\" title=\"Wayne Gretzky\">Wayne Gretzky</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Aaron_Judge\" title=\"Aaron Judge\">Aaron Judge</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Shaquille_O%27Neal\" title=\"Shaquille O'Neal\">Shaquille O'Neal</a> acted as draw assistants.<sup id=\"cite_ref-4\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-4\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>4<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p>The draw was attended by the coaches and representatives from 64 teams — the 42 qualified teams and the 22 teams participating in either the inter-confederation or the European playoffs.<sup id=\"cite_ref-5\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-5\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>5<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> Also in attendance were Canadian prime minister <a href=\"/wiki/Mark_Carney\" title=\"Mark Carney\">Mark Carney</a>, Mexican president <a href=\"/wiki/Claudia_Sheinbaum\" title=\"Claudia Sheinbaum\">Claudia Sheinbaum</a>, and American president <a href=\"/wiki/Donald_Trump\" title=\"Donald Trump\">Donald Trump</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-6\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-6\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>6<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p><a href=\"/wiki/Iran\" title=\"Iran\">Iran</a> had initially announced that it would boycott the ceremony after <a href=\"/wiki/Mehdi_Taj\" title=\"Mehdi Taj\">Mehdi Taj</a>, the president of the <a href=\"/wiki/Football_Federation_Islamic_Republic_of_Iran\" title=\"Football Federation Islamic Republic of Iran\">Football Federation Islamic Republic of Iran</a> (FFIRI), was denied a visa to enter the United States.<sup id=\"cite_ref-7\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-7\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>7<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> However, the FFIRI later stated that it had reversed its decision, and would be sending representatives led by coach <a href=\"/wiki/Amir_Ghalenoei\" title=\"Amir Ghalenoei\">Amir Ghalenoei</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-8\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-8\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>8<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p>Prior to the draw, FIFA head <a href=\"/wiki/Gianni_Infantino\" title=\"Gianni Infantino\">Gianni Infantino</a> presented the very first <b>FIFA Peace Prize</b> to <a href=\"/wiki/U.S._President\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"U.S. President\">U.S. President</a> Donald Trump.<sup id=\"cite_ref-Guardian-main_9-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-Guardian-main-9\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>9<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-AP_10-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-AP-10\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>10<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-SkyNews_11-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-SkyNews-11\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>11<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> FIFA stated that the award recognized his \"involvement in ceasefire efforts and diplomatic engagements in several conflicts\".<sup id=\"cite_ref-FIFA-announcement_12-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-FIFA-announcement-12\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>12<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-Dawn_13-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-Dawn-13\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>13<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-RNZ_14-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-RNZ-14\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>14<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The creation of the prize and the choice of Trump as its first recipient generated widespread ridicule, scrutiny and controversy. Human rights groups, analysts, and soccer stakeholders questioned the transparency of the selection process, the suitability of the inaugural laureate, and the implications both for FIFA's claims of political neutrality and its human rights commitments.<sup id=\"cite_ref-AJ-Analysis_15-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-AJ-Analysis-15\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>15<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-Amnesty_16-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-Amnesty-16\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>16<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-NewHumanitarian_17-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-NewHumanitarian-17\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>17<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-18\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-18\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>18<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Seeding\">Seeding</h2><span class=\"mw-editsection\"><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">[</span><a href=\"/w/index.php?title=2026_FIFA_World_Cup_draw&amp;action=edit&amp;section=2\" title=\"Edit section: Seeding\"><span>edit</span></a><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">]</span></span></div>\n<p>The 42 known teams were seeded based on the <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_Men%27s_World_Ranking\" title=\"FIFA Men's World Ranking\">FIFA Men's World Ranking</a> of November 19, 2025 (shown in parentheses).<sup id=\"cite_ref-19\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-19\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>19<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The six unknown placeholders representing the playoff winners were automatically placed in Pot 4.<sup id=\"cite_ref-20\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-20\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>20<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<table class=\"wikitable\" style=\"white-space:nowrap\">\n<caption>Pots\n</caption>\n<tbody><tr>\n<th width=\"25%\">Pot 1\n</th>\n<th width=\"25%\">Pot 2\n</th>\n<th width=\"25%\">Pot 3\n</th>\n<th width=\"25%\">Pot 4\n</th></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><div><ol style=\"margin-left: 1.5em;\"><li><span class=\"flagicon\"><span class=\"mw-image-border\" typeof=\"mw:File\"><span><img alt=\"\" class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>A<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-22\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-22\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>B<span id=\"cite_ref-placeholder_21-5\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-placeholder-21\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>A<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-23\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-23\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>C<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li></ol></div>\n</td></tr></tbody></table>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Draw_constraints_and_procedure\">Draw constraints and procedure</h2><span class=\"mw-editsection\"><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">[</span><a href=\"/w/index.php?title=2026_FIFA_World_Cup_draw&amp;action=edit&amp;section=3\" title=\"Edit section: Draw constraints and procedure\"><span>edit</span></a><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">]</span></span></div>\n<p>Two teams from the same confederation could not be placed in the same group, with the exception of <a href=\"/wiki/UEFA\" title=\"UEFA\">UEFA</a> for which there had to be at least one but no more than two teams in each group. The confederation restriction applied to all three potential winners of the inter-confederation playoffs. FIFA also announced that, \"in the interest of ensuring competitive balance\", two separate pathways to the semifinals (sides of the knockout bracket) were established. Based on this, the teams ranked first (<a href=\"/wiki/Spain_national_football_team\" title=\"Spain national football team\">Spain</a>) and second (<a href=\"/wiki/Argentina_national_football_team\" title=\"Argentina national football team\">Argentina</a>) in the ranking were randomly drawn into groups in opposite pathways, as were the teams ranked third (<a href=\"/wiki/France_national_football_team\" title=\"France national football team\">France</a>) and fourth (<a href=\"/wiki/England_national_football_team\" title=\"England national football team\">England</a>). Therefore, should these pairs of teams win their groups, they will be unable to meet until the final, while all four will be unable to meet until the semifinals.\n</p><p>Despite the procedure stating that the three co-hosts would be identified by different colored balls and placed in pre-defined positions as per the match schedule, the draw for the co-hosts was instead performed by their respective heads of government, who drew their own nations out. The draw then started with the remaining nine teams in Pot 1 who were identified by nine balls of the same color and allocated to position 1 of the group into which they were drawn. For pots 2, 3 and 4, each team's group position was determined according to a predefined allocation pattern, which was published by FIFA as a part of the draw procedures as follows:<sup id=\"cite_ref-24\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-24\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>21<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-25\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-25\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>22<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<ul><li>Groups A, D, G, J\n<ul><li>Position 2: Team from Pot 3</li>\n<li>Position 3: Team from Pot 2</li>\n<li>Position 4: Team from Pot 4</li></ul></li>\n<li>Groups B, E, H, K\n<ul><li>Position 2: Team from Pot 4</li>\n<li>Position 3: Team from Pot 3</li>\n<li>Position 4: Team from Pot 2</li></ul></li>\n<li>Groups C, F, I, L\n<ul><li>Position 2: Team from Pot 2</li>\n<li>Position 3: Team from Pot 4</li>\n<li>Position 4: Team from Pot 3</li></ul></li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Final_draw\">Final draw</h2><span class=\"mw-editsection\"><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">[</span><a href=\"/w/index.php?title=2026_FIFA_World_Cup_draw&amp;action=edit&amp;section=4\" title=\"Edit section: Final draw\"><span>edit</span></a><span class=\"mw-editsection-bracket\">]</span></span></div>\n<p>The twelve groups were formed randomly, selecting one team from each of the four pots. The three hosts' positions in the draw was predetermined – Mexico in A1, Canada in B1, and the United States in D1. Unlike previous editions, the position of unseeded teams in each group was also predetermined as opposed to being randomly drawn.\n</p>\n<style data-mw-deduplicate=\"TemplateStyles:r1349610755\">.mw-parser-output .defaultleft,.mw-parser-output .col1left td:nth-child(1),.mw-parser-output .col2left td:nth-child(2),.mw-parser-output .col3left class=\"theo-social-text-container\"></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><p class=\"article-section_previewText__sxFP5\">Find out how the 12 sections took shape as the world watched and some huge names descended on the US capital.</p><div class=\"article-section_richText__ohRhO rich-text_richText__HXCSS ff-mb-md-1024-24 article-section_articleLink__8y41+\"><p class=\"rich-text_p__UfX5b article-section_articleParagraph__pYCnd text-regular\">The <a href=\"https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026\">FIFA World Cup 2026™</a> group stage has been mapped out during a dazzling Final Draw at the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts in Washington&nbsp;DC.</p><p class=\"rich-text_p__UfX5b article-section_articleParagraph__pYCnd text-regular\">Brazil-Morocco, Netherlands-Japan and a rematch between France and 2002 conquerors Senegal were among the standout fixtures to emerge, while debutants Cabo Verde, Curaçao, Jordan and Uzbekistan can look forward to mouth-watering match-ups against Spain, Germany, Argentina and Portugal respectively. Mexico-South Africa was also confirmed as the tournament's Opening Match in Mexico City on Thursday, 11 June 2026.</p><p class=\"rich-text_p__UfX5b article-section_articleParagraph__pYCnd text-regular\">The next major milestone on the road to 2026 will come when the <a href=\"https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/articles/updated-match-schedule-live-in-washington-dc-6-december\">updated World Cup 2026 match schedule is unveiled live on FIFA.com on Saturday, 6 December</a>, with venues and kick-off times for all 104 matches confirmed at 12:00 EST (18:00 CET) in Washington, DC. </p><p class=\"rich-text_p__UfX5b article-section_articleParagraph__pYCnd text-regular\">The 12 groups below were drawn in an event conducted by former England captain Rio Ferdinand, with sporting icons Tom Brady, Wayne Gretzky, Aaron Judge and Shaquille O’Neal serving as assistants. Kevin Hart, Heidi Klum and Danny Ramirez hosted a star-studded draw show that opened with a spine-tingling performance by Andrea Bocelli and also saw Robbie&nbsp;Williams, Nicole&nbsp;Scherzinger, Lauryn Hill and the Village People entertain the watching world.</p><hr class=\"rich-text_hr__2C63+ article-section_articleHr__BchCk\"><h4 class=\"ff-text-blue-dark article-section_headings__iQUD7\"><span class=\"d-md-none\"><span title=\"&quot;World Cup 2026 groups: How teams qualify and tie-breakers&quot;\" style=\"display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 5; id=\"ot-pvcy-hdr\">Your Privacy</h4><p id=\"ot-pc-desc\" class=\"ot-grp-desc\">When you visit any website, it may store or retrieve information on your browser, mostly in the form of cookies. This information might be about you, your preferences or your device and is mostly used to make the site work as you expect it to. The information does not usually directly identify you, but it can give you a more personalized web experience. Because we respect your right to privacy, you can choose to opt out of some types of cookies. Click on the different category headings to find out more and change our default settings. However, opting out of some types of cookies may impact your experience of the site and the services we are able to offer. Using the link below, you can contact our Data Protection team to place a do not sell or share my personal information request:\n            <br><a href=\"mailto:dataprotection@fifa.org\" class=\"privacy-notice-link\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\" aria-label=\"Do not sell my personal data, opens in a new tab\">Do not sell my personal data</a></p></div><div class=\"ot-desc-cntr ot-hide ot-always-active-group\" role=\"tabpanel\" id=\"ot-desc-id-1\"><div class=\"ot-grp-hdr1\"><h4 class=\"ot-cat-header\">Strictly Necessary Cookies</h4><div class=\"ot-tgl-cntr\"><div class=\"ot-always-active\">Always Active</div></div></div><p class=\"ot-grp-desc ot-category-desc\">These cookies are necessary for the website to function and cannot be switched off in our systems. data-react-component=\"ShowMoreText\" data-expanded=\"false\">\n\t\t\t\t\t\t<p>Telemundo Deportes’ Andres Cantor breaks down the USMNT’s chances in Group D after the FIFA World Cup draw.</p>\n\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</figcaption>\n\t\t\t</figure>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t<div class=\"article-content rich-text\">\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\n\t\t\t\t\t\n<p>The stage is almost set for the biggest spectacle in soccer.</p>\n\n<p>The <a href=\"/news/sports/fifa-world-cup-draw-spectacle-bigger/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer noopener\">2026 FIFA World Cup draw</a> was held Friday at the <a href=\"/news/sports/soccer/2026-world-cup-draw-trump-kennedy-center-december/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer noopener\">Kennedy Center in Washington, D.C.</a>, to map out the groups for an unprecedented tournament.</p>\n\n<p>Next summer's event will be the <a href=\"/news/sports/soccer/2026-fifa-world-cup-format-explained/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer noopener\">first ever 48-team World Cup</a>, with those nations being broken up into 12 groups of four. From there, 104 matches will be played at 16 venues across the U.S., Canada and Mexico to determine a World Cup champion.</p>\n<div class=\"mobile-app-promotion\">\n\t\n<div class=\"wp-block-nbc-html-block html-block\"><div class=\"wp-block-nbc-html-block html-block\"><div id=\"ctaConWatch\" style=\"background-color: #edf3fb;padding: 15px 15px\"> <p style=\"font-size: teams have already qualified for the tournament, with the final six spots to be decided by <a href=\"/news/sports/soccer/fifa-world-cup-playoff-draw-europe-intercontinental/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer noopener\">UEFA and interconfederation playoffs</a> in March.</p>\n\n<p>Here is a look at all 12 groups now that the draw is complete, along with the opponents for each of the host New format explained\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</a>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</h3>\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n\t\n<h2 class=\"wp-block-nbc-section-heading\">When is the 2026 FIFA World Cup?</h2>\n\n<p>The 2026 World Cup will go from Thursday, June 11, through Sunday, July 19.</p>\n\n<h2 class=\"wp-block-nbc-section-heading\">When does the 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule come out?</h2>\n\n<p>The full schedule with each match's teams, stadium and kickoff time will be announced at 12 p.m. ET on Saturday, Dec. 6.</p>\n\n<div class=\"wp-block-nbc-html-block html-block\"><script type=\"text/javascript\" charset=\"UTF-8\" src=\"https://telemundowashingtondc.com/portableplayer/?CID=1:34:2357170&amp;videoID=2473124931930&amp;origin=telemundowashingtondc.com&amp;fullWidth=y\"></script><iframe width=\"100%\" height=\"415.125\" scrolling=\"no\" id=\"nbcLMP134235717019\" allowfullscreen=\"true\" webkitallowfullscreen=\"true\" mozallowfullscreen=\"true\" src=\"https://telemundowashingtondc.com/video-layout/amp_video/?noid=1:34:2357170&amp;videoID=2473124931930&amp;origin=telemundowashingtondc.com&amp;fullWidth=y&amp;turl=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nbcnewyork.com%2Fnews%2Fsports%2Fsoccer%2Ffifa-world-cup-draw-2026%2F6426378%2F&amp;ourl=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nbcnewyork.com&amp;lp=5&amp;fullWidth=y&amp;random=cvshhq&amp;callletters=wzdc&amp;embedded=true&amp;autoplay=false\" style=\"border-width: medium; border-style: none; border-color: currentcolor; border-image: initial;\"></iframe></div>\n\n<h2 class=\"wp-block-nbc-section-heading\">What group is the U.S. men's national team in?</h2>\n\n<p>The <a href=\"/news/sports/soccer/usmnt-group-d-draw-fifa-world-cup-2026/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noreferrer noopener\">U.S. men's national team</a> is in Group D with Australia, Paraguay and the winner of a European playoff between Turkey, Romania, Slovakia and Kosovo.</p>\n\n<h2 class=\"wp-block-nbc-section-heading\">When will the USMNT play?</h2>\n\n<p>While the full match schedule for the World Cup will not be announced until Saturday, the USMNT has known the dates and venues for its group stage matches since February 2024. With the draw complete, the USMNT knows its schedule, as well:</p>\n\n<ul class=\"wp-block-list\">\n<li><strong>Match 1:</strong> Friday, June 12 – USMNT vs. Paraguay, Los Angeles Stadium</li>\n\n\n\n<li><strong>Match 2: </strong>Friday, June 19 – USMNT vs. Australia, Seattle Stadium</li>\n\n\n\n<li><strong>Match 3: </strong>Thursday, June 25 – USMNT vs. Winner UEFA Playoff C, Los Angeles Stadium</li>\n</ul>\n<div data-react-component=\"VideoPlaylist\" Death.\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t</div>\n<h2 class=\"wp-block-nbc-section-heading\">What groups are Mexico and Canada in?</h2>\n\n<p>The United States' North American co-hosts, Mexico and Canada, also know their opponents, venues and dates for their group stage matches.</p>\n\n<p><strong>Mexico (Group A)</strong></p>\n\n<ul class=\"wp-block-list\">\n<li><strong>Match 1:</strong> Thursday, June 11 – Mexico vs. South Africa, Mexico City Stadium</li>\n\n\n\n<li><strong>Match 2: </strong>Thursday, June 18 – Mexico vs. South Korea, Estadio Guadalajara</li>\n\n\n\n<li><strong>Match 3: </strong>Wednesday, June 24 – Mexico vs. Winner UEFA Playoff D, Mexico City Stadium</li>\n</ul>\n\n<p><strong>Canada (Group B)</strong></p>\n\n<ul class=\"wp-block-list\">\n<li><strong>Match 1:</strong> Friday, June 12 – Canada vs. Winner UEFA Playoff A, Toronto Stadium</li>\n\n\n\n<li><strong>Match 2: </strong>Thursday, June 18 – Canada vs. Qatar, BC Place Vancouver</li>\n\n\n\n<li><strong>Match 3: </strong>Wednesday, June 24 – Canada vs. Switzerland, BC Place Vancouver</li>\n</ul>\n\n<h2 class=\"wp-block-nbc-section-heading\">2026 FIFA World Cup matches by host city</h2>\n\n<p>Here is a breakdown of all <a close this box, so please open the Opt-Out Form first. </b> </li></ul><p></p> \n<p><b><u>Please note: </u></b>Opting out will also prevent disclosure of personal information to our <a href=\"https://corporate.comcast.com/privacy-related-businesses\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\">Related Businesses</a> (except as necessary for the provision of services or when you class=\"mw-content-ltr mw-parser-output\" lang=\"en\" dir=\"ltr\"><div class=\"shortdescription nomobile noexcerpt noprint searchaux\" style=\"display:none\">Association football tournament</div>\n<p class=\"mw-empty-elt\">\n\n</p>\n<style data-mw-deduplicate=\"TemplateStyles:r1358620234\">@media(min-width:640px){.mw-parser-output td{padding-left:inherit;padding-right:inherit}}</style><table class=\"infobox vcalendar\"><caption class=\"infobox-title summary\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification</caption><tbody><tr><td colspan=\"2\" class=\"infobox-image\"><span class=\"mw-default-size\" typeof=\"mw:File/Frameless\"><a href=\"/wiki/File:2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_logo.png\" class=\"mw-file-description\"><img confederations)</td></tr><tr><th colspan=\"2\" class=\"infobox-header\">Tournament statistics</th></tr><tr><th scope=\"row\" class=\"infobox-label\">Matches&nbsp;played</th><td class=\"infobox-data\">899</td></tr><tr><th scope=\"row\" class=\"infobox-label\">Goals scored</th><td class=\"infobox-data\">2,527&nbsp;(2.81 per match)</td></tr><tr><th scope=\"row\" class=\"infobox-label\">Attendance</th><td class=\"infobox-data\">15,626,580&nbsp;(17,382 per match)</td></tr><tr><th scope=\"row\" class=\"infobox-label\">Top scorer</th><td class=\"infobox-data href=\"/wiki/Erling_Haaland\" title=\"Erling Haaland\">Erling Haaland</a> (16 goals)</td></tr><tr><td colspan=\"2\" class=\"infobox-below\" style=\"border-top:#bbb solid 1px\"><div style=\"float: left;\">← <a href=\"/wiki/2022_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification\" title=\"2022 FIFA World Cup qualification\">2022</a> </div><div style=\"float: right;\"><i><a href=\"/wiki/2030_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"2030 FIFA World Cup qualification\">2030</a></i> → </div></td></tr></tbody></table><div class=\"shortdescription nomobile noexcerpt noprint searchaux\" style=\"display:none\">International football competition</div>\n<p>The <b>2026 FIFA World Cup qualification</b> decided the 45 teams that joined hosts <a href=\"/wiki/Canada_men%27s_national_soccer_team\" title=\"Canada men's national soccer team\">Canada</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Mexico_national_football_team\" title=\"Mexico national football team\">Mexico</a>, and the <a href=\"/wiki/United_States_men%27s_national_soccer_team\" title=\"United States men's national soccer team\">United States</a> at the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup\">2026 FIFA World Cup</a>.\n</p><p>The <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_World_Cup_qualifiers\" title=\"FIFA World Cup qualifiers\">qualifying process</a> began on 7 September 2023 with three matches of <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(CONMEBOL)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONMEBOL)\">the CONMEBOL zone</a> played that day.<sup id=\"cite_ref-1\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-1\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>1<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The first goal of the qualification series was scored by <a href=\"/wiki/Colombia_national_football_team\" title=\"Colombia national football team\">Colombian</a> player <a href=\"/wiki/Rafael_Santos_Borr%C3%A9\" title=\"Rafael Santos Borré\">Rafael Santos Borré</a> against <a href=\"/wiki/Venezuela_national_football_team\" title=\"Venezuela national football team\">Venezuela</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-2\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-2\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>2<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-3\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-3\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>3<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> Qualification ended on 31 March 2026.\n</p><p><a href=\"/wiki/Cape_Verde_national_football_team\" title=\"Cape Verde national football team\">Cape Verde</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Cura%C3%A7ao_national_football_team\" title=\"Curaçao national football team\">Curaçao</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Jordan_national_football_team\" title=\"Jordan national football team\">Jordan</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Uzbekistan_national_football_team\" title=\"Uzbekistan national football team\">Uzbekistan</a> all qualified for the first time, with Curaçao becoming the smallest nation to ever qualify with a population of just 158,000,<sup id=\"cite_ref-4\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-4\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>4<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-5\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-5\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>5<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> while <a href=\"/wiki/Qatar_national_football_team\" title=\"Qatar national football team\">Qatar</a> had their first successful qualifying campaign after making their debut in the previous edition as hosts, and <a href=\"/wiki/Iraq_national_football_team\" title=\"Iraq national football team\">Iraq</a> had the longest qualifying campaign, playing 21 matches over a 28-month period to qualify for the World Cup.<sup id=\"cite_ref-6\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-6\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>6<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-7\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-7\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>7<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<meta property=\"mw:PageProp/toc\">\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Slot_allocation\">Slot allocation</h2></div>\n<p>On 30 March 2017, the Bureau of the <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_Council\" title=\"FIFA Council\">FIFA Council</a> (composed of the FIFA president and the presidents of each of the six confederations) proposed a slot allocation for the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The recommendation was submitted for the ratification by the FIFA Council.<sup id=\"cite_ref-slot_8-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-slot-8\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>8<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-9\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-9\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>9<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p>On 9 May 2017, two days before the 67th <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_Congress\" title=\"FIFA Congress\">FIFA Congress</a>, the FIFA Council approved the slot allocation in a meeting in <a href=\"/wiki/Manama\" title=\"Manama\">Manama</a>, Bahrain. This included an intercontinental play-off tournament involving six teams to decide the last two FIFA World Cup spots.<sup id=\"cite_ref-fifa_council_10-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-fifa_council-10\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>10<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p>The ratification of slot allocation also gave the <a href=\"/wiki/Oceania_Football_Confederation\" title=\"Oceania Football Confederation\">OFC</a> a guaranteed berth in the final tournament for the first time in FIFA World Cup history; as such, the 2026 FIFA World Cup became the first tournament in which all six confederations have at least one guaranteed berth.\n</p>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Qualified_teams\">Qualified teams</h2></div>\n<figure class=\"mw-halign-center\" typeof=\"mw:File/Thumb\"><a href=\"/wiki/File:2026_world_cup_qualification_map.svg\" class=\"mw-file-description\"><img src=\"//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/2026_world_cup_qualification_map.svg/960px-2026_world_cup_qualification_map.svg.png\" decoding=\"async\" width=\"824\" height=\"363\" class=\"mw-file-element\" srcset=\"//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/2026_world_cup_qualification_map.svg/1920px-2026_world_cup_qualification_map.svg.png 2x\" data-file-width=\"2756\" data-file-height=\"1214\"></a><figcaption><style data-mw-deduplicate=\"TemplateStyles:r981673959\">.mw-parser-output data-mw-group=\"lower-alpha\">\n<li id=\"cite_note-Hosts-12\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\">^ <a href=\"#cite_ref-Hosts_12-0\"><sup><i><b>a</b></i></sup></a> <a href=\"#cite_ref-Hosts_12-1\"><sup><i><b>b</b></i></sup></a> <a href=\"#cite_ref-Hosts_12-2\"><sup><i><b>c</b></i></sup></a></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Although the <a href=\"/wiki/United_2026_FIFA_World_Cup_bid\" title=\"United 2026 FIFA World Cup bid\">United bid</a> was selected on 13 June 2018, the automatic berths allocated to the hosts were not confirmed until a decision by the <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_Council\" title=\"FIFA Council\">FIFA Council</a> on 14 February 2023.<sup id=\"cite_ref-11\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-11\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>11<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-13\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-13\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Until 1991, Uzbekistan was part of the <a href=\"/wiki/Soviet_Union_national_football_team\" title=\"Soviet Union national football team\">Soviet Union</a>, which competed at seven World Cup tournaments. FIFA considers <a href=\"/wiki/Russia_national_football_team\" title=\"Russia national football team\">Russia</a> to be the successor team of the Soviet Union.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-14\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-14\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">The <a href=\"/wiki/1934_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1934 FIFA World Cup\">1934 World Cup</a> was a straight knockout tournament between 16 teams and did not feature a group stage.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-15\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-15\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">France qualified 18 times, but only participated 17 times as they withdrew in <a href=\"/wiki/1950_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1950 FIFA World Cup\">1950</a>.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-16\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-16\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Until 1992, Croatia was part of <a href=\"/wiki/Yugoslavia_national_football_team\" title=\"Yugoslavia national football team\">Yugoslavia</a>, which competed at eight World Cup tournaments. FIFA considers <a href=\"/wiki/Serbia_national_football_team\" title=\"Serbia national football team\">Serbia</a> to be the successor team of Yugoslavia.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-17\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-17\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">From 1950 to 1990, Germany competed as West Germany, as a separate <a href=\"/wiki/East_Germany_national_football_team\" title=\"East Germany national football team\">East German team</a> existed during that period.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-18\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-18\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Scotland qualified 10 times, but only participated 9 times as they withdrew in <a href=\"/wiki/1950_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1950 FIFA World Cup\">1950</a>.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-19\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-19\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Austria qualified 9 times, but only participated 8 times as they withdrew in <a href=\"/wiki/1938_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1938 FIFA World Cup\">1938</a>.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-20\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-20\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Until 1992, Bosnia and Herzegovina was part of <a href=\"/wiki/Yugoslavia_national_football_team\" title=\"Yugoslavia national football team\">Yugoslavia</a>, which competed at eight World Cup tournaments. FIFA considers <a href=\"/wiki/Serbia_national_football_team\" title=\"Serbia national football team\">Serbia</a> to be the successor team of Yugoslavia.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-21\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-21\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Turkey qualified 4 times, but only participated 3 times as they withdrew in <a href=\"/wiki/1950_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1950 FIFA World Cup\">1950</a>.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-22\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-22\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Includes eight appearances by <a href=\"/wiki/Czechoslovakia_national_football_team\" title=\"Czechoslovakia national football team\">Czechoslovakia</a>, as FIFA considers <a href=\"/wiki/Czech_Republic_national_football_team\" title=\"Czech Republic national football team\">Czech Republic</a> and <a href=\"/wiki/Slovakia_national_football_team\" title=\"Slovakia national football team\">Slovakia</a> to be joint successors.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-23\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-23\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">From 1970 to 1998, DR Congo competed as Zaire.</span>\n</li>\n</ol></div></div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"Qualified_teams_facts\">Qualified teams facts</h3></div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Debutants_and_returnees\">Debutants and returnees</h4></div>\n<ul><li><a href=\"/wiki/Cape_Verde_national_football_team\" title=\"Cape Verde national football team\">Cape Verde</a>,<sup id=\"cite_ref-24\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-24\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>12<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> <a href=\"/wiki/Cura%C3%A7ao_national_football_team\" title=\"Curaçao national football team\">Curaçao</a>,<sup id=\"cite_ref-25\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-25\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>A<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-:2_26-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-:2-26\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>13<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> <a href=\"/wiki/Jordan_national_football_team\" title=\"Jordan national football team\">Jordan</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Uzbekistan_national_football_team\" title=\"Uzbekistan national football team\">Uzbekistan</a><sup id=\"cite_ref-27\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-27\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>B<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-28\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-28\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>14<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> all made their World Cup debuts.</li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Qatar_national_football_team\" title=\"Qatar national football team\">Qatar</a> advanced to the tournament through qualification for the first time, as it automatically qualified as host in 2022.<sup id=\"cite_ref-29\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-29\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>15<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/DR_Congo_national_football_team\" title=\"DR Congo national football team\">DR Congo</a><sup id=\"cite_ref-30\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-30\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>C<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> and <a href=\"/wiki/Haiti_national_football_team\" title=\"Haiti national football team\">Haiti</a> returned to the tournament after appearing in their only previous tournament in <a href=\"/wiki/1974_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1974 FIFA World Cup\">1974</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-:2_26-1\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-:2-26\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>13<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-31\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-31\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>16<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Iraq_national_football_team\" title=\"Iraq national football team\">Iraq</a> returned to the tournament after appearing in their only previous tournament in <a href=\"/wiki/1986_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1986 FIFA World Cup\">1986</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-32\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-32\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>17<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Austria_national_football_team\" title=\"Austria national football team\">Austria</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Norway_national_football_team\" title=\"Norway national football team\">Norway</a>,<sup id=\"cite_ref-33\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-33\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>18<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> and <a href=\"/wiki/Scotland_national_football_team\" title=\"Scotland national football team\">Scotland</a> return to the tournament after last appearing in <a href=\"/wiki/1998_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1998 FIFA World Cup\">1998</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-34\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-34\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>19<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Turkey_national_football_team\" title=\"Turkey national football team\">Turkey</a> qualified for the first time since finishing third in <a href=\"/wiki/2002_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2002 FIFA World Cup\">2002</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-35\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-35\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>20<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Czech_Republic_national_football_team\" title=\"Czech Republic national football team\">Czech Republic</a><sup id=\"cite_ref-36\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-36\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>D<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> qualified for the first time since <a href=\"/wiki/2006_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2006 FIFA World Cup\">2006</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-37\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-37\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>21<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/New_Zealand_men%27s_national_football_team\" title=\"New Zealand men's national football team\">New Zealand</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Paraguay_national_football_team\" title=\"Paraguay national football team\">Paraguay</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/South_Africa_national_soccer_team\" title=\"South Africa national soccer team\">South Africa</a> (2010 World Cup host) returned after last taking part in <a href=\"/wiki/2010_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2010 FIFA World Cup\">2010</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-38\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-38\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>22<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> South Africa advanced to the tournament through qualification for the first time since <a href=\"/wiki/2002_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2002 FIFA World Cup\">2002</a>, while New Zealand was the lowest ranked team to qualify, ranked 85th.<sup id=\"cite_ref-39\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-39\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>23<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Algeria_national_football_team\" title=\"Algeria national football team\">Algeria</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Bosnia_and_Herzegovina_national_football_team\" title=\"Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team\">Bosnia and Herzegovina</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Ivory_Coast_national_football_team\" title=\"Ivory Coast national football team\">Ivory Coast</a> qualified for the first time since <a href=\"/wiki/2014_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2014 FIFA World Cup\">2014</a>. The Bosnians achieved their first successful qualification process for a major tournament since the 2014 World Cup.</li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Colombia_national_football_team\" title=\"Colombia national football team\">Colombia</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Egypt_national_football_team\" title=\"Egypt national football team\">Egypt</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Panama_national_football_team\" title=\"Panama national football team\">Panama</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Sweden_men%27s_national_football_team\" title=\"Sweden men's national football team\">Sweden</a> made a return after missing out in <a href=\"/wiki/2022_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2022 FIFA World Cup\">2022</a>.</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Absentees\">Absentees</h4></div>\n<ul><li><a href=\"/wiki/Italy_national_football_team\" title=\"Italy national football team\">Italy</a> missed out after being defeated in the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_UEFA_second_round#Path_A\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA second round\">European playoff final</a> by Bosnia and Herzegovina on <a href=\"/wiki/Penalty_shootout\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"Penalty shootout\">penalties</a>, becoming the first former champion to miss out on three consecutive World Cups.<sup id=\"cite_ref-40\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-40\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>24<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> Similar to <a href=\"/wiki/2018_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2018 FIFA World Cup\">2018</a> and 2022, Italy was the only former champion that did not qualify.</li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Chile_national_football_team\" title=\"Chile national football team\">Chile</a>, who last qualified in 2014, failed to advance for the third consecutive time, in a similar way to Italy, being the second time the country did not qualify to three consecutive World Cups after failing from 1986 to 1994.</li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Nigeria_national_football_team\" title=\"Nigeria national football team\">Nigeria</a>, who last qualified in 2018, failed to advance after losing to DR Congo on penalties in the final match of the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_CAF_second_round\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF second round\">CAF play-offs</a>. This marked the first time the country failed to qualify for consecutive World Cups since before their first successful qualification in <a href=\"/wiki/1994_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1994 FIFA World Cup\">1994</a>.</li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Cameroon_national_football_team\" title=\"Cameroon national football team\">Cameroon</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Costa_Rica_national_football_team\" title=\"Costa Rica national football team\">Costa Rica</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Denmark_national_football_team\" title=\"Denmark national football team\">Denmark</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Poland_national_football_team\" title=\"Poland national football team\">Poland</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Serbia_national_football_team\" title=\"Serbia national football team\">Serbia</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Wales_national_football_team\" title=\"Wales national football team\">Wales</a>, all of whom qualified in 2022, also did not qualify for the 2026 tournament.</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Other\">Other</h4></div>\n<ul><li>Uzbekistan will become the first country from <a href=\"/wiki/Central_Asia\" title=\"Central Asia\">Central Asia</a> to take part in the FIFA World Cup.<sup id=\"cite_ref-41\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-41\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>25<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li>Iraq's successful qualification campaign for the 2026 World Cup is considered one of the longest ever by a national team, spanning 21 matches over a period of 28 months.<sup id=\"cite_ref-42\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-42\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>26<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-43\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-43\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>27<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li>This was the first time that two <a href=\"/wiki/Caribbean\" title=\"Caribbean\">Caribbean</a> nations (Curaçao and Haiti) qualified for the World Cup.</li>\n<li>This was the first time that eight <a href=\"/wiki/Arab_world\" title=\"Arab world\">Arab nations</a> (Algeria, Egypt, Iraq, Jordan, Morocco, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and Tunisia) qualified for the World Cup, an increase from four teams in 2018 and 2022.<sup id=\"cite_ref-44\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-44\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>28<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li><a href=\"/wiki/Sweden_men%27s_national_football_team\" title=\"Sweden men's national football team\">Sweden</a> advanced to the tournament via the play-offs, becoming the first European nation to qualify for the World Cup after only reaching the play-offs through the <a href=\"/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_UEFA_Nations_League_C\" title=\"2024–25 UEFA Nations League C\">UEFA Nations League</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-45\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-45\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>29<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li>\n<li>As of 1 April 2026, the highest ranked team not to qualify was Italy (ranked 12th), while the lowest ranked team that did qualify was New Zealand (ranked 85th).</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Qualification_process\">Qualification process</h2></div>\n<p>On 9 May 2017, the FIFA Council approved the <a href=\"#Slot_allocation\">slot allocation</a> scheme for the new 48-team final format.<sup id=\"cite_ref-fifa_council_10-1\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-fifa_council-10\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>10<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"Summary_of_qualification\">Summary of qualification</h3></div>\n<table class=\"wikitable\" style=\"text-align: center;\">\n\n<tbody><tr>\n<td colspan=\"10\" style=\"background:white\"><span typeof=\"mw:File\"><a href=\"/wiki/File:World_Map_FIFA2.svg\" class=\"mw-file-description\"><img src=\"//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/World_Map_FIFA2.svg/500px-World_Map_FIFA2.svg.png\" decoding=\"async\" width=\"350\" height=\"155\" class=\"mw-file-element\" srcset=\"//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/World_Map_FIFA2.svg/960px-World_Map_FIFA2.svg.png 2x\" data-file-width=\"940\" data-file-height=\"415\"></a></span>\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<th width=\"100\">Confederation\n</th>\n<th width=\"80\">Direct slots\n</th>\n<th width=\"80\">Change from 2022\n</th>\n<th width=\"80\"><span class=\"nowrap\">Play-off</span> slots\n</th>\n<th (hosts)</td>\n<td>29</td>\n<td>3+3 (hosts)</td>\n<td>22 March 2024</td>\n<td>18 November 2025\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(CONMEBOL)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONMEBOL)\">CONMEBOL</a></td>\n<td>6</td>\n<td>+2</td>\n<td>1</td>\n<td>10</td>\n<td>4</td>\n<td>6</td>\n<td>7 September 2023</td>\n<td>9 September 2025\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(OFC)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)\">OFC</a></td>\n<td>1</td>\n<td>+1</td>\n<td>1</td>\n<td>11</td>\n<td>10</td>\n<td>1</td>\n<td>6 September 2024</td>\n<td>24 March 2025\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(UEFA)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)\">UEFA</a></td>\n<td>16</td>\n<td>+3</td>\n<td>0</td>\n<td>54</td>\n<td>38</td>\n<td>16</td>\n<td>21 March 2025</td>\n<td>31 March 2026\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><i><a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\">Play-offs</a></i></td>\n<td>2</td>\n<td>—</td>\n<td data-sort-value=\"\" style=\"background: var(--background-color-interactive, #ececec); color: var(--color-base, inherit); vertical-align: middle; text-align: center;\" class=\"table-na\"><span aria-hidden=\"true\">—</span><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1152813436\"><span class=\"sr-only\">N/a</span></td>\n<td>(6)</td>\n<td>(4)</td>\n<td>(2)</td>\n<td>26 March 2026</td>\n<td>31 March 2026\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<th>Total</th>\n<th>45+3 (hosts)</th>\n<th>+16</th>\n<th>6</th>\n<th>206+3 (hosts)</th>\n<th>161</th>\n<th>45+3</th>\n<th>7 September 2023</th>\n<th>31 March 2026\n</th></tr></tbody></table>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"Suspensions_and_withdrawals\">Suspensions and withdrawals</h3></div>\n<p><a href=\"/wiki/Russia_national_football_team\" title=\"Russia national football team\">Russia</a> were suspended indefinitely on 28 February 2022 from participating in UEFA and FIFA competitions due to <a href=\"/wiki/2022_Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine\" title=\"2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine\">the country's invasion of Ukraine</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-46\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-46\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>30<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> A circular distributed by UEFA on 11 November 2024 stated 192 group stage matches would take place, confirming Russia's exclusion from the tournament.<sup id=\"cite_ref-47\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-47\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>31<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p><a href=\"/wiki/Sri_Lanka_national_football_team\" title=\"Sri Lanka national football team\">Sri Lanka</a> were suspended in January 2023 for an unspecified reason,<sup id=\"cite_ref-48\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-48\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>32<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> and they did not appear in the draw information the AFC released in early July.<sup id=\"cite_ref-49\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-49\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>33<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> However, the suspension was lifted on the condition that their federation would hold elections at least ten days before qualification would begin.<sup id=\"cite_ref-50\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-50\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>34<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p><a href=\"/wiki/Eritrea_national_football_team\" title=\"Eritrea national football team\">Eritrea</a> withdrew from qualification on 10 November 2023, prior to playing any matches, due to concerns that players would seek political asylum if allowed to travel overseas.<sup id=\"cite_ref-51\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-51\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>35<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-52\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-52\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>36<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p><a href=\"/wiki/Congo_national_football_team\" title=\"Congo national football team\">Congo</a> were suspended on 6 February 2025 for government interference in <a href=\"/wiki/Congolese_Football_Federation\" title=\"Congolese Football Federation\">Congolese Football Federation</a> operations.<sup id=\"cite_ref-53\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-53\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>37<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-54\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-54\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>38<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> No announcement regarding their status was immediately available, and CAF initially cancelled their remaining matches.<sup id=\"cite_ref-55\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-55\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>39<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The suspension was lifted by FIFA on 14 May 2025,<sup id=\"cite_ref-56\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-56\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>40<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> and Tanzania and Zambia were awarded 3–0 victories by forfeit.<sup id=\"cite_ref-57\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-57\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>41<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Format\">Format</h2></div>\n<p>Each confederation was responsible for its own qualification tournament, which consisted of at least one round of competition using the following formats (Regulations Article 11.3):<sup id=\"cite_ref-regulations_58-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-regulations-58\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>42<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<ul><li>A double <a href=\"/wiki/Round-robin_tournament\" title=\"Round-robin tournament\">round-robin</a> (or \"league\") format in which each team in a group played all other teams in their group twice – once at home and once away.</li>\n<li>A single round-robin format in which each team in a group played all other teams in their group once, with the venues either drawn at random or assigned by the confederation with the agreement of the playing associations.</li>\n<li>A <a href=\"/wiki/Single-elimination_tournament\" title=\"Single-elimination tournament\">knockout format</a> in which each team in a round played one other team over <a href=\"/wiki/Two-legged_tie\" title=\"Two-legged tie\">two legs</a> – once at home and once away.</li>\n<li>With FIFA's permission, a tournament held in one of the participating nations or in neutral territory.</li>\n<li>With FIFA's permission, a single-leg knockout format.</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"Tiebreakers\">Tiebreakers</h3></div>\n<p>In the round-robin and tournament formats, the tiebreaker criteria were as follows (Regulations Article 11.5):<sup id=\"cite_ref-regulations_58-1\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-regulations-58\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>42<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<div><ol><li>Greatest number of points obtained in all group matches (3 for a win, 1 for a draw, 0 for a loss)</li><li>Greatest goal differential in all group matches</li><li>Most goals scored in all group matches</li></ol></div>\n<p>Should two or more teams still have equal rank after criteria 1–3 were applied, then the following criteria were used:\n</p>\n<div><ol start=\"4\"><li>Greatest number of points obtained in all matches amongst the concerned teams</li><li>Greatest goal differential in all matches amongst the concerned teams</li><li>Most goals scored in all matches amongst the concerned teams</li><li>Most goals scored away from home in all matches amongst the concerned teams</li><li>The best team conduct score using the following table:\n<div><ol style=\"list-style-type:lower-alpha\"><li>First yellow card: –1 point</li><li>Second yellow card/indirect red card: –3 points</li><li>Direct red card: –4 points</li><li>Yellow card and direct red card: –5 points</li></ol></div>\nOnly one score was applied to any player or team official in any match, e.g. a player who received a second yellow card would only have –3 points applied and not –4 (–1 for the first and –3 for the second).</li><li>Drawing of lots by FIFA</li></ol></div>\n<p>If the tournament were held in a single host nation or in neutral territory, then criterion 7 would not be applied (Regulations Article 11.6).<sup id=\"cite_ref-regulations_58-2\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-regulations-58\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>42<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p>In the home-and-away knockout format, the team scoring more goals on aggregate won the tie and advanced. If the aggregate score were level, then 30 minutes of <a href=\"/wiki/Overtime_(sports)#Association_football\" title=\"Overtime (sports)\">extra time</a> would be played. If the score were still level at the end of extra time, the <a href=\"/wiki/Away_goals_rule\" title=\"Away goals rule\">away goals rule</a> would not be applied and the winners would be decided by a <a href=\"/wiki/Penalty_shoot-out_(association_football)\" title=\"Penalty shoot-out (association football)\">penalty shoot-out</a> (Regulations Article 11.9).<sup id=\"cite_ref-regulations_58-3\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-regulations-58\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>42<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> In the <span class=\"nowrap\">single-leg</span> knockout format, the team scoring more goals in a single match wins and advances. If the teams were tied after regulation, then extra time was played, followed by a penalty shoot-out if the score was still level (Regulations Article 11.10).<sup id=\"cite_ref-regulations_58-4\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-regulations-58\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>42<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Confederation_qualification\">Confederation qualification</h2></div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"AFC\">AFC</h3></div>\n<style data-mw-deduplicate=\"TemplateStyles:r1353705441\">.mw-parser-output .hatnote{font-style:italic}.mw-parser-output div.hatnote{padding-left:1.6em;margin-bottom:0.5em}.mw-parser-output .hatnote i{font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .hatnote+span.mw-empty-elt+.hatnote,.mw-parser-output .hatnote+link+.hatnote{margin-top:-0.5em}@media print{body.ns-0 .mw-parser-output .hatnote{display:none!important}}</style><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(AFC)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)</a></div>\n<p>On 1 August 2022, the <a href=\"/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation\" title=\"Asian Football Confederation\">Asian Football Confederation</a> Executive Committee approved the qualification format for Asia's road to the 2026 World Cup, as well as the <a href=\"/wiki/2027_AFC_Asian_Cup\" title=\"2027 AFC Asian Cup\">2027 AFC Asian Cup</a>, in preparation for the eight direct spots and the single intercontinental <span class=\"nowrap\">play-off</span> slot allocated to the AFC by FIFA following the expansion of the FIFA World Cup to 48 teams.<sup id=\"cite_ref-2026_AFC_Qualification_Pathway_59-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-2026_AFC_Qualification_Pathway-59\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>43<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The draw for the first round was held on 27 July 2023 at the AFC House in <a href=\"/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur\" title=\"Kuala Lumpur\">Kuala Lumpur</a>, Malaysia.<sup id=\"cite_ref-July_format_60-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-July_format-60\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>44<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The draw for the second round took place two hours later.\n</p><p>The qualification structure was as follows:\n</p>\n<ul><li><b>First round</b>: Twenty teams (ranked 27–46) played <span class=\"nowrap\">home-and-away</span> over <a href=\"/wiki/Two-legged_tie\" title=\"Two-legged tie\">two legs</a>.<sup id=\"cite_ref-new_format_61-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-new_format-61\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>45<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The ten winners advanced to the second round.</li>\n<li><b>Second round</b>: Thirty-six teams (those ranked 1–26 and the ten <span class=\"nowrap\">first-round</span> winners) were divided into nine groups of four teams to play <span class=\"nowrap\">home-and-away</span> <a href=\"/wiki/Round-robin_tournament\" title=\"Round-robin tournament\"><span class=\"nowrap\">round-robin</span></a> matches. The eighteen group winners and group <span class=\"nowrap\">runners-up</span> advanced to the third round.</li>\n<li><b>Third round</b>: The eighteen teams that advanced from the second round were divided into three groups of six teams to play <span class=\"nowrap\">home-and-away</span> <span class=\"nowrap\">round-robin</span> matches. The top two teams of each group qualified for the World Cup, while the <span class=\"nowrap\">third-placed</span> and <span class=\"nowrap\">fourth-placed</span> teams of each group advanced to the fourth round.</li>\n<li><b>Fourth round</b>: The six teams that advanced from the third round were drawn into two groups of three teams each to play a single <span class=\"nowrap\">round-robin</span>. The winners qualified for the World Cup, and the runners-up advanced to the fifth round.</li>\n<li><b>Fifth round</b>: The fourth round group runners-up competed in a <a href=\"/wiki/Two-legged_tie\" title=\"Two-legged tie\">two-legged</a> <span class=\"nowrap\">play-off</span> tie that determined the Asian representation at the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\"><span class=\"nowrap\">inter-confederation</span> <span class=\"nowrap\">play-offs</span></a>.</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Final_positions_(third_round)\"><span id=\"Final_positions_.28third_round.29\"></span>Final positions (third round)</h4></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_AFC_third_round\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC third round\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC third round</a></div>\n<table class=\"wikitable\" style=\"font-size:85%\">\n\n<tbody><tr>\n<th scope=\"col\">Legend\n</th></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959\"><div class=\"legend\"><span class=\"legend-color mw-no-invert\" style=\"background-color:#BBF3BB; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;\">&nbsp;</span>&nbsp;Qualified for the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup\">2026 FIFA World Cup</a></div>\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959\"><div class=\"legend\"><span class=\"legend-color mw-no-invert\" style=\"background-color:#BBF3FF; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;\">&nbsp;</span>&nbsp;Advanced to the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_AFC_fourth_round\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – AFC fourth round\">fourth round</a></div>\n</td></tr></tbody></table>\n<table>\n\n<tbody><tr style=\"vertical-align:top\">\n<td style=\"width:25em\"><style data-mw-deduplicate=\"TemplateStyles:r1011077906\">.mw-parser-output round\">1–2</a></td></tr></tbody></table>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"CAF\">CAF</h3></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(CAF)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)</a></div>\n<p>The CAF Executive Committee announced a new African qualification format on 19 May 2023.<sup id=\"cite_ref-ExCon_62-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-ExCon-62\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>46<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-format_63-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-format-63\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>47<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> The draw was held on 13 July 2023 in <a href=\"/wiki/Abidjan\" title=\"Abidjan\">Abidjan</a>, Ivory Coast.<sup id=\"cite_ref-64\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-64\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>48<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-draw_65-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-draw-65\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>49<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> All 54 FIFA-affiliated football associations from CAF entered qualification. Eritrea withdrew before matches began due to fears that players would seek political asylum abroad.<sup id=\"cite_ref-66\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-66\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>50<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-67\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-67\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>51<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p>The qualification structure was as follows:\n</p>\n<ul><li><b>First round</b>: Teams were drawn into nine groups of six teams to play home-and-away <a href=\"/wiki/Round-robin_tournament\" title=\"Round-robin tournament\">round-robin</a> matches. The winner of each group qualified for the World Cup.</li>\n<li><b>Second round</b>: The four best-ranked group runners-up participated in a play-off to determine which team would advance to the <a class=\"mw-selflink-fragment\" href=\"#Inter-confederation_play-offs\">inter-confederation play-offs</a>.</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Final_positions_(first_round)\"><span id=\"Final_positions_.28first_round.29\"></span>Final positions (first round)</h4></div>\n<table class=\"wikitable\" style=\"font-size:85%\">\n\n<tbody><tr>\n<th scope=\"col\">Legend\n</th></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959\"><div class=\"legend\"><span class=\"legend-color mw-no-invert\" style=\"background-color:#BBF3BB; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;\">&nbsp;</span>&nbsp;Qualified for the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup\">2026 FIFA World Cup</a></div>\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959\"><div class=\"legend\"><span class=\"legend-color mw-no-invert\" style=\"background-color:#BBF3FF; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;\">&nbsp;</span>&nbsp;Advanced to the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_CAF_second_round\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF second class=\"external text\" href=\"https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/qualifiers/caf/standings\">FIFA</a></div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Final_positions_(second_round)\"><span id=\"Final_positions_.28second_round.29\"></span>Final positions (second round)</h4></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_CAF_second_round\" href=\"/wiki/FIFA_Men%27s_World_Ranking\" title=\"FIFA Men's World Ranking\">FIFA Men's World Ranking</a> of 17 October 2025, with the highest-ranked side taking on the lowest and the second-highest taking on the third-highest.<sup id=\"cite_ref-83\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-83\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>67<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-84\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-84\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>68<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-85\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\">inter-confederation play-offs</a>.\n</p>\n<table cellpadding=\"0\" cellspacing=\"0\" style=\"margin:.9em 2em 1em 1em;border:0;font-size:90%;border-collapse:separate;white-space:nowrap\"><tbody><tr><td><div style=\"height:1ex;overflow:hidden\">&nbsp;</div></td></tr></tbody></table>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"CONCACAF\">CONCACAF</h3></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(CONCACAF)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)</a></div>\n<p>Three teams in CONCACAF—Canada, Mexico and the United States—automatically qualified as host nations. On 28 February 2023, CONCACAF announced the qualifying format for 2026 World Cup qualification.<sup id=\"cite_ref-CONCACAF_format_86-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-CONCACAF_format-86\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>70<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<ul><li><b>First round</b>: Four CONCACAF teams, ranked 29 to 32 based on the <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_World_Ranking\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"FIFA World Ranking\">FIFA ranking</a> of December 2023, were divided into two matchups to be played on a <a href=\"/wiki/Two-legged_tie\" title=\"Two-legged tie\"><span class=\"nowrap\">two-legged</span></a> <span class=\"nowrap\">home-and-away</span> basis. The two winners advanced to the second round.</li>\n<li><b>Second round</b>: Thirty teams – the two winners from the first round and CONCACAF teams ranked 1 to 28 based on the FIFA ranking of December 2023 – were drawn into six groups of five teams. They played single <a href=\"/wiki/Round-robin_tournament\" title=\"Round-robin tournament\"><span class=\"nowrap\">round-robin</span></a> matches (two home and two away), with group winners and <span class=\"nowrap\">runners-up</span> advancing to the third round.</li>\n<li><b>Third round</b>: The twelve teams that advanced from the second round were drawn into three groups of four teams. They played double round-robin home-and-away matches, with the three group winners qualifying for the World Cup. The two <span class=\"nowrap\">best-ranked</span> <span class=\"nowrap\">runners-up</span> advanced to the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\"><span class=\"nowrap\">inter-confederation</span> <span class=\"nowrap\">play-offs</span></a>.</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Final_positions_(third_round)_2\"><span id=\"Final_positions_.28third_round.29_2\"></span>Final positions (third round)</h4></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_CONCACAF_third_round\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF third round\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF third round</a></div>\n<table class=\"wikitable\" style=\"font-size:85%\">\n\n<tbody><tr>\n<th scope=\"col\">Legend\n</th></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\"
