href=\"https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/qualifiers/concacaf/standings\">FIFA</a></div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"CONMEBOL\">CONMEBOL</h3></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(CONMEBOL)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONMEBOL)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONMEBOL)</a></div>\n<p>On 22 August 2022, CONMEBOL petitioned FIFA to keep the qualification format which had been used since the <a href=\"/wiki/1998_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(CONMEBOL)\" title=\"1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONMEBOL)\">1998 FIFA World Cup qualification</a>, where all CONMEBOL members play <span class=\"nowrap\">home-and-away</span> <a href=\"/wiki/Round-robin_tournament\" title=\"Round-robin tournament\"><span class=\"nowrap\">round-robin</span></a> matches against each other.<sup id=\"cite_ref-87\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-87\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>71<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-88\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-88\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>72<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-CONCACAF_number_89-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-CONCACAF_number-89\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>73<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup><sup id=\"cite_ref-90\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-90\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>74<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> This was approved, with the first games of the qualifiers played in September 2023.<sup id=\"cite_ref-91\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-91\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>75<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p><p>Before the qualification competition began, <a href=\"/wiki/Ecuador_national_football_team\" title=\"Ecuador national football team\">Ecuador</a> were penalized 3 points for using falsified birth documents for <a href=\"/wiki/Byron_Castillo\" title=\"Byron Castillo\">Byron Castillo</a> in the previous World Cup qualification cycle.<sup id=\"cite_ref-ecuador_deduction_92-0\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-ecuador_deduction-92\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>76<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Final_positions\">Final positions</h4></div>\n<table class=\"wikitable\" style=\"font-size:85%\">\n\n<tbody><tr>\n<th scope=\"col\">Legend\n</th></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959\"><div class=\"legend\"><span class=\"legend-color mw-no-invert\" style=\"background-color:#BBF3BB; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;\">&nbsp;</span>&nbsp;Qualified for the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup\">2026 FIFA World Cup</a></div>\n</td></tr>\n<tr>\n<td><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959\"><div class=\"legend\"><span class=\"legend-color mw-no-invert\" style=\"background-color:#BBF3FF; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;\">&nbsp;</span>&nbsp;Advanced to the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\">inter-confederation play-offs</a></div>\n</td></tr></tbody></table>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1011077906\">\n<table class=\"wikitable\" style=\"text-align:center;\">\n<tbody><tr>\n<th scope=\"col\" width=\"28\"><abbr title=\"Position\">Pos</abbr>\n</th>\n<th scope=\"col\" width=\"190\">Team<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1333133064\"><link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1239400231\"><div class=\"navbar plainlinks hlist navbar-mini\" style=\"float:right\"><ul class=\"navbar-brackets\"><li class=\"nv-view\"><a href=\"/wiki/Template:2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_%E2%80%93_CONMEBOL_table\" href=\"https://www.fifa.com/en/tournaments/mens/worldcup/canadamexicousa2026/qualifiers/conmebol/standings\">FIFA</a></div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"OFC\">OFC</h3></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(OFC)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)</a></div>\n<p>The 2026 World Cup marked the first time OFC was granted one guaranteed slot, as well as a possible second slot via the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\">inter-confederation play-offs</a>.\n</p><p>The qualification structure was as follows:<sup id=\"cite_ref-93\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-93\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>77<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<ul><li><b>First round</b>: The four lowest-ranked teams played a <span class=\"nowrap\">three-match</span> knockout round in September 2024. The winner, Samoa, advanced to the second round.</li>\n<li><b>Second round</b>: The winning team from the first round joined the seven <span class=\"nowrap\">highest-ranked</span> teams in two four-team groups in October and November 2024. The top two teams from each group advanced to the third round.</li>\n<li><b>Third round</b>: The four teams advancing from the second round played a <span class=\"nowrap\">three-match</span> knockout round in March 2025. The winner, New Zealand, qualified for the World Cup, and the runner-up, New Caledonia, advanced to the <span class=\"nowrap\">inter-confederation</span> <span class=\"nowrap\">play-offs</span>.</li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Final_positions_(third_round)_3\"><span id=\"Final_positions_.28third_round.29_3\"></span>Final positions (third round)</h4></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(OFC)#Third_round\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (OFC) §&nbsp;Third round</a></div>\n<p>The winner qualified for the World Cup, while the runner-up advanced to the <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\">inter-confederation play-offs</a>.\n</p>\n<table cellpadding=\"0\" cellspacing=\"0\" style=\"margin:.9em 2em 1em mw-heading3\"><h3 id=\"UEFA\">UEFA</h3></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(UEFA)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)</a></div>\n<p>The UEFA Executive Committee announced a new European qualification format on 25 January 2023. Teams were drawn into twelve groups of four or five teams to play home-and-away <a href=\"/wiki/Round-robin_tournament\" title=\"Round-robin tournament\">round-robin</a> matches from March to November 2025. The group winners qualified for the World Cup, while the <span class=\"nowrap\">runners-up</span> will participate in <span class=\"nowrap\">play-off</span> matches,<sup id=\"cite_ref-94\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-94\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>78<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> for a total of 16 teams qualifying for the finals.\n</p><p>The qualification draw took place on 13 December 2024 in <a href=\"/wiki/Z%C3%BCrich\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"Zürich\">Zürich</a>, Switzerland.<sup id=\"cite_ref-95\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-95\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>79<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup> Due to the <a href=\"/wiki/Russian_invasion_of_Ukraine\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"Russian invasion of Ukraine\">Russian invasion of Ukraine</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Russia_national_football_team\" title=\"Russia national football team\">Russia's national team</a> was suspended, and their exclusion was confirmed in a circular distributed by UEFA on 11 November 2024.<sup id=\"cite_ref-96\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-96\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>80<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<ul><li><b>First round (group stage)</b>: Twelve groups of either four or five teams were drawn, with group winners qualifying for the World Cup.</li>\n<li><b>Second round (play-off)</b>: Sixteen teams (twelve group runners-up and the four best Nations League group winners, based on the <a href=\"/wiki/2024%E2%80%9325_UEFA_Nations_League#Overall_ranking\" title=\"2024–25 UEFA Nations League\">2024–25 Nations League overall ranking</a>, that finished outside the top two of their qualifying group) were drawn into four <span class=\"nowrap\">play-off</span> paths, playing two rounds of <span class=\"nowrap\">single-match</span> <span class=\"nowrap\">play-offs</span> (<span class=\"nowrap\">semi-finals</span> with the seeded teams hosting, followed by finals, with the home teams drawn from the semi-final pairings). The four path winners qualified for the World Cup.<sup id=\"cite_ref-97\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-97\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>81<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup></li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading4\"><h4 id=\"Final_positions_(first_round)_2\"><span id=\"Final_positions_.28first_round.29_2\"></span>Final style=\"height:1ex;overflow:hidden\">&nbsp;</div></td></tr></tbody></table>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Inter-confederation_play-offs\">Inter-confederation play-offs</h2></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441\"><div role=\"note\" class=\"hatnote navigation-not-searchable\">Main article: <a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(inter-confederation_play-offs)\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)\">2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (inter-confederation play-offs)</a></div>\n<p>A play-off tournament involving six teams – one each from <a href=\"/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation\" title=\"Asian Football Confederation\">AFC</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/Confederation_of_African_Football\" title=\"Confederation of African Football\">CAF</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/CONMEBOL\" title=\"CONMEBOL\">CONMEBOL</a>, and <a href=\"/wiki/Oceania_Football_Confederation\" title=\"Oceania Football Confederation\">OFC</a> and two from <a href=\"/wiki/CONCACAF\" title=\"CONCACAF\">CONCACAF</a> – was held to decide two FIFA World Cup berths. The bottom four teams in the November 2025 <a href=\"/wiki/FIFA_World_Rankings\" class=\"mw-redirect\" title=\"FIFA World Rankings\">FIFA World Rankings</a> were drawn into two <span class=\"nowrap\">single-elimination</span> matches. The winners of those matches advanced to play the top two teams in a further round of <span class=\"nowrap\">single-elimination</span> matches, and the winners qualified for the World Cup. The play-offs were held in March 2026 in <a href=\"/wiki/Guadalajara\" title=\"Guadalajara\">Guadalajara</a> and <a href=\"/wiki/Monterrey\" title=\"Monterrey\">Monterrey</a> in Mexico.<sup id=\"cite_ref-98\" class=\"reference\"><a href=\"#cite_note-98\"><span class=\"cite-bracket\">[</span>82<span class=\"cite-bracket\">]</span></a></sup>\n</p>\n<table cellpadding=\"0\" goalscorer lists for all confederations and the inter-confederation play-offs:\n</p>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1333133064\"><div class=\"hlist nowraplinks\">\n<ul><li><a href=\"/wiki/2026_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(AFC)#Top_goalscorers\" title=\"2026 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)\">AFC</a></li>\n<li><a class=\"nowrap\">Inter-confederation play-offs</span></a></li></ul>\n</div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"See_also\">See also</h2></div>\n<ul><li><a href=\"/wiki/2027_FIFA_Women%27s_World_Cup_qualification\" title=\"2027 FIFA Women's World Cup qualification\">2027 FIFA Women's World Cup qualification</a></li></ul>\n<div class=\"mw-heading mw-heading2\"><h2 id=\"Notes\">Notes</h2></div>\n<link rel=\"mw-deduplicated-inline-style\" href=\"mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1327269900\"><div>\n<div class=\"mw-references-wrap\"><ol class=\"references\" data-mw-group=\"upper-alpha\">\n<li id=\"cite_note-25\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-25\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Curaçao is the smallest country by area and the least populous to qualify for the World Cup. Excluding teams from the <a href=\"/wiki/United_Kingdom\" title=\"United Kingdom\">United Kingdom</a>, Curaçao is also the first team representing a non-sovereign nation to qualify for the World Cup since the Dutch East Indies (currently <a href=\"/wiki/Indonesia_at_the_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"Indonesia at the FIFA World Cup\">Indonesia</a>) in <a href=\"/wiki/1938_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1938 FIFA World Cup\">1938</a>.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-27\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-27\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Until 1991, Uzbekistan was part of the <a href=\"/wiki/Soviet_Union_national_football_team\" title=\"Soviet Union national football team\">Soviet Union</a>, which competed at seven World Cup tournaments. Following the <a href=\"/wiki/Dissolution_of_the_Soviet_Union\" title=\"Dissolution of the Soviet Union\">dissolution of the Soviet Union</a>, Uzbekistan became the third former Soviet republic to compete as an independent nation after <a href=\"/wiki/Russia_national_football_team\" title=\"Russia national football team\">Russia</a> (<a href=\"/wiki/1994_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"1994 FIFA World Cup\">1994</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/2002_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2002 FIFA World Cup\">2002</a>, <a href=\"/wiki/2014_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2014 FIFA World Cup\">2014</a> and <a href=\"/wiki/2018_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2018 FIFA World Cup\">2018</a>) and <a href=\"/wiki/Ukraine_national_football_team\" title=\"Ukraine national football team\">Ukraine</a> (<a href=\"/wiki/2006_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2006 FIFA World Cup\">2006</a>). FIFA considers Russia to be the successor team of the Soviet Union.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-30\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-30\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">From 1970 to 1998, DR Congo competed as <a href=\"/wiki/Zaire\" title=\"Zaire\">Zaire</a>. This was the first time the country competed under DR Congo.</span>\n</li>\n<li id=\"cite_note-36\"><span class=\"mw-cite-backlink\"><b><a href=\"#cite_ref-36\">^</a></b></span> <span class=\"reference-text\">Until 1992, the Czech Republic was part of Czechoslovakia, which competed in eight World Cup tournaments. Following <a href=\"/wiki/Dissolution_of_Czechoslovakia\" title=\"Dissolution of Czechoslovakia\">its dissolution</a>, the Czech Republic has qualified for the tournament for the second time as an independent nation, having previously debuted in <a href=\"/wiki/2006_FIFA_World_Cup\" title=\"2006 FIFA World Cup\">2006</a>.</span>\n</li>\n</ol></div></div>\n<div class=\"mw-heading
